2605.00607.title: Beyond Decodability: Reconstructing Language Model Representations with an Encoding Probe
2605.00607.paper_summary: Probing is widely used to study which features can be decoded from language model representations. However, the common decoding probe approach has two limitations that we aim to solve with our new encoding probe approach: contributions of different features to model representations cannot be directly compared, and feature correlations can affect probing results. We present an Encoding Probe that reverses this direction and reconstructs internal representations of models using interpretable features. We evaluate this method on text and speech transformer models, using feature sets spanning acoustics, phonetics, syntax, lexicon, and speaker identity. Our results suggest that speaker-related effects vary strongly across different training objectives and datasets, while syntactic and lexical features contribute independently to reconstruction. These results show that the Encoding Probe provides a complementary perspective on interpreting model representations beyond decodability.
2605.00607.paper_summary_zh: 探測技術廣泛用於研究哪些特徵可以從語言模型的表示中解碼。然而，常見的解碼探測方法有兩個限制，我們的新編碼探測方法旨在解決這些限制：不同特徵對模型表示的貢獻無法直接比較，且特徵相關性可能影響探測結果。我們提出了一種編碼探測器，它逆轉了這一方向，並利用可解釋的特徵重建模型的內部表示。我們在文本和語音Transformer模型上評估這種方法，使用的特徵集涵蓋聲學、語音學、語法、詞彙和說話者身份。我們的結果表明，與說話者相關的效果在不同的訓練目標和數據集之間變化很大，而語法和詞彙特徵則獨立地對重建作出貢獻。這些結果顯示，編碼探測器提供了一種補充的視角，用於解釋模型表示，超越了可解碼性。
2605.00607.author: Gaofei Shen et.al.
2605.00607.authors: Gaofei Shen, Martijn Bentum, Tom Lentz, Afra Alishahi, Grzegorz Chrupała
